questions.question: 1. What are the majority charge carriers of a p-type semiconductor and the minority charge carriers of an n-type semiconductor?

explanations.a: This is incorrect. In a p-type semiconductor, holes (not electrons) are the majority carriers. In an n-type semiconductor, electrons are the majority carriers, not the minority carriers.
explanations.b: This is correct. In a p-type semiconductor, holes are the majority carriers due to acceptor doping. In an n-type semiconductor, holes are the minority carriers because electrons are the majority carriers.
explanations.c: This is incorrect. While holes are the majority carriers in a p-type semiconductor, electrons (not holes) are the minority carriers in an n-type semiconductor.
explanations.d: This is incorrect. Electrons are not the majority carriers in a p-type semiconductor, and holes are not the minority carriers in an n-type semiconductor.

questions.question: 2. A 1D semiconductor of length l , placed along positive x axis (0 at left and L at right), is uniformly doped with p-type dopants for 0 ≤ x ≤ l/2 and undoped for l/2 ≤ x ≤ l. What will the direction of Drift current at x=L/2 will be?

answers.d: Drift current direction cannot be determined

explanations.a: This is incorrect. The electric field points from the undoped region (positive) to the p-type region (negative). Holes, being positively charged, move in the opposite direction of the electric field, i.e., from right to left.
explanations.b: This is correct. The electric field points from the undoped region to the p-type region, and holes move in the opposite direction, i.e., from right to left.
explanations.c: This is incorrect. There is an electric field at x=L/2 due to the doping gradient, and this field drives a drift current.
explanations.d: This is incorrect. The direction of the drift current can be determined based on the doping gradient and the resulting electric field.

questions.question: 3. Will the Fermi function in the semiconductor in the previous question be continuous at length x = l/2 under equilibrium?

answers.a: Yes, the Fermi function will drop by kt/q at the junction.
answers.b: No, the Fermi function will have a discontinuity at x = l/2 due to the doping change.
answers.c: Yes, because the Fermi level adjusts to maintain equilibrium across the boundary.
answers.d: No, because the undoped region has no free carriers to align the Fermi level.

explanations.a: This is incorrect. The Fermi function is not always continuous at material boundaries when there is an abrupt change in doping or material properties.
explanations.b: This is incorrect. While the Fermi level adjusts in equilibrium, the abrupt doping change at x = l/2 can cause a discontinuity in the Fermi function.
explanations.c: This is correct. The Fermi function will have a discontinuity at x = l/2 because the doping changes abruptly, leading to a mismatch in the Fermi levels of the doped and undoped regions.
explanations.d: This is incorrect. The undoped region does have some free carriers (intrinsic carriers), but the abrupt doping change still causes a discontinuity in the Fermi function.

questions.question: 4. Now the same semiconductor is doped with n-type dopants from length x = l/2 to x = l. How will the net diffusion current change?

answers.a: The diffusion current will increase due to the higher concentration gradient of electrons.
answers.b: The diffusion current will decrease because the doping is now uniform.
answers.c: The diffusion current will remain the same as there is no change in the doping profile.
answers.d: The diffusion current will decrease due to the absence of holes.

explanations.a: The rate of diffusion depends on the concentration gradient of charge carriers. Adding n-type dopants from x = l/2 to x = l creates a higher concentration gradient of electrons, increasing the rate of diffusion.
explanations.b: This is incorrect. The doping is not uniform; there is a transition from p-type to n-type doping, creating a concentration gradient.
explanations.c: This is incorrect. The doping profile changes significantly, as the semiconductor now has p-type doping in one region and n-type doping in the other.
explanations.d: This is incorrect. While holes are absent in the n-type region, the rate of diffusion is determined by the concentration gradient of electrons, which increases.
